channel.title: INtech
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from INtech, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: The Shoes and Brooms Transforming Curling at the 2026 Winter Olympics
item.description: Halfway between chess and bocce, curling is experiencing an evolution thanks to technology. From brooms to stones to footwear, here’s everything you n...

item.title: Best Vacuum Cleaner (2026): Cordless Vacuums, Robot Vacuums, Dysons
item.description: Looking for all our top recommended vacuums? Here are our favorites in every style we’ve tested, from stick vacs to robot vacuums.

item.title: The kids ‘picked last in gym class’ gear up for Super Bowl
item.description: YouTube CEO Neal Mohan is expected to be there. Apple's Tim Cook, too.

item.title: I Have Fallen in Love With Open Earbuds (and You Should Too)
item.description: From jogging and cycling to multitasking or puttering around the house, open earbuds are an excellent way to jam out in the real world.

item.title: Best Wireless Earbuds (2026): Apple, Sony, Bose, and More
item.description: Ready to cut the cord? These are our favorite buds that will never, ever get tangled.

item.title: 4 Best AI Notetakers (2026), Tested and Reviewed
item.description: A growing collection of pocket-sized gadgets lets you record your meetings and extract value from them. Here are our favorites.

item.title: India has changed its startup rules for deep tech
item.description: India is adjusting startup rules to help more of its deep tech startups with funding and long-term success.

item.title: From Svedka to Anthropic, brands make bold plays with AI in Super Bowl ads
item.description: From the first AI-generated Big Game ad courtesy of Svedka to Anthropic's beef with OpenAI, here are the biggest ads from Super Bowl LX.

item.title: NBA star Giannis Antetokounmpo joins Kalshi as an investor
item.description: Antetokounmpo is the first NBA player to directly invest in Kalshi.

item.title: New York lawmakers propose a three-year pause on new data centers
item.description: Although the bill’s prospects are uncertain, New York is at least the sixth state to consider pausing construction of new data centers.
